title: improve-directory-structure: a structural/dependency architecture skill
summary: A grill session evaluated whether the existing improve-codebase-architecture skill (module-depth lens, assumes CONTEXT.md/ADRs, testability-oriented heuristics) is the right tool to reorganize and clean up the claude-code-shared tooling repo itself (29 skills, 18 agents, 21 scripts, contracts, resources). Investigation found the skill is language-agnostic but its depth/testability heuristics and context-loader grounding assumptions don't map cleanly onto a prose/config-driven infra repo with no CONTEXT.md or ADRs. Rather than bending that skill to fit, the session designed a new sibling skill, improve-directory-structure, scoped to structural/organizational/dependency cleanup for any directory with structured references (code or not). It uses a deterministic scanner script (not freehand exploration) to build a node/edge dependency graph via pluggable per-repo-type extractors, evaluates findings against a curated cohesion/coupling/dependency-direction litmus set (SRP, CCP, CRP, DRY, ADP, SDP), stops at a verified plan/seed (no direct execution), and folds reference-integrity checking into the same scanner as a --check mode. Module-depth analysis and routing/decision-quality evaluation are explicitly deferred to the existing improve-codebase-architecture and improve-skill skills respectively.
decisions: Primary target for this effort is structural/organizational health (file placement, coupling, consolidation, hierarchy weight) in the claude-code-shared tooling repo, not runtime decision-quality (routing, model-tier choices). Decision-quality is an explicit secondary/later pass using improve-skill.

Before any architecture work runs against claude-code-shared, seed a thin CONTEXT.md for the tooling system: a noun glossary (skill, agent, contract, hook, resource, registry, pipeline stage, tier, etc.) plus load-bearing invariants (absolute-path script references, registry.json as source-of-truth for agents, model-tiers.json keying skills/agents by name, .cch/.cco symlinking into claude-code-shared, hooks wired via settings.json). This grounds context-loader, which otherwise returns empty vocabulary/adrs when CONTEXT.md and docs/adr/ are absent.

improve-codebase-architecture stays scoped to code/module depth (React/TS and other executable-code targets). It is not the tool for reorganizing the claude-code-shared prose/config tooling repo. Do not bend or overload it to cover both use cases.

Build a new, separate sibling skill for structural/organizational/dependency cleanup rather than extending improve-codebase-architecture. It must reuse existing shared infra (context-loader agent, grill-step pattern, to-seed handoff) rather than duplicating it, to avoid adding to hierarchy bloat.

The new skill's scope is shape (b): audit through plan. It diagnoses and produces a verified reorg plan/seed. It does not execute file moves or reference rewrites directly -- those are handed to the existing to-seed -> to-tasks -> build-code pipeline as discrete, scriptable tasks.

The new skill's primary lens is a dependency/placement graph (nodes = skills/agents/scripts/contracts/resources; edges = absolute-path script calls, registry.json file refs, model-tiers.json keys, subagent_type spawns, resource includes, skill-pipeline.json stage links), not module-depth. Module-depth (the deletion test, shallow-interface analysis) is demoted to a secondary lens applied only to nodes already flagged by the graph.

The dependency graph must be built by a dedicated, deterministic scanner script living in claude-code-shared/scripts/ (per the standing scriptable-code rule), not by freehand Grep/Explore each run. The scanner emits {nodes, edges, metrics} JSON (fan-in, fan-out, dir depth, orphan flags); the skill reasons over that JSON rather than re-deriving it.

The scanner architecture splits into a generic, repo-agnostic analysis core (consumes {nodes, edges, metrics} JSON, computes coupling/placement/consolidation/hierarchy findings) plus pluggable per-repo-type edge extractors (the only repo-specific part). Ship two extractors at launch: one for this claude-tooling repo's edge conventions (registry.json, subagent_type=, absolute script paths, model-tiers.json keys) and one generic-code extractor (import/require-style edges across common languages). A markdown/docs-link extractor is deferred to a follow-on, not built now.

The skill's use-case boundary is 'any directory with structured, explicit cross-references' (code imports, config registries, doc link graphs) -- not literally any folder. Sparse/unstructured prose trees are out of the productive use case and should be described honestly as such, mirroring how improve-codebase-architecture is honestly scoped to module depth.

The quality litmus for reorg findings is a curated subset of classic architecture principles bound to concrete, computable graph signals, not the broad Clean Code canon: SRP (fan-out spans unrelated clusters -> split), CCP/locality (edges all point to one owner but node sits in shared -> move next to owner), CRP (resource used by one consumer but bucketed as shared -> co-locate or inline), DRY (high edge/content similarity between two nodes -> consolidate), ADP (a cycle in the edge graph -> break it), SDP (churny high-fan-out node depended on by many -> stabilize or invert), and a coupling/cohesion cluster-modularity score (a directory is really two concerns). Code-mechanics concerns (naming, function length, comments, error handling) are explicitly out of scope for this skill -- they belong to improve-codebase-architecture / improve-component. Every finding produced by the skill must cite both the principle and the specific graph edge/evidence that proves the violation.

Reference-integrity validation after a reorg is folded into the same scanner as a --check mode, rather than building a standalone validator. The scanner already resolves every edge to build the graph, so --check reuses that resolution to assert zero dangling edges and no new cycles, exiting non-zero on failure. This is dogfooding the DRY principle the skill itself enforces. The check mode must follow .cch/.cco symlinks (not treat them as ordinary edges) and must detect literal absolute-path string references (e.g. ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/scripts/x.sh), not just structured registry entries. Every reorg task emitted downstream gets scanner --check as its acceptance gate.

The new skill is named improve-directory-structure (chosen over improve-repo-structure because 'repo' implies a git repository and would narrow the already-agreed any-directory, non-code-inclusive scope; 'directory' matches what the scanner literally takes as input).

The new skill is registered via /register-skill so it appears in model-tiers.json, the weekly usage report, and sync-model-tiers. The skill itself (judgment: reading the graph, grilling the reorg, writing the plan) is tiered T4/opus, matching the other architecture and grill skills. The scanner script and both extractors are deterministic, tier-less scripts (like validate_registry.py), not model-tiered entries. The existing context-loader agent is reused as-is rather than duplicated, now that a seeded CONTEXT.md will let it return real vocabulary.
next_action: Run /to-tasks on this seed to break the improve-directory-structure skill build into independently-gradable tasks (CONTEXT.md seeding, scanner script + --check mode, two edge extractors, generic analysis core, SKILL.md authoring, /register-skill registration).
